title: The Kiosk That Compared Itself

features.name: the mirror-screen
features.desc-on-touch: A display showing the council kiosk's own display, showing the same display, showing you looking. The recursion is shallow and readable, three iterations deep, then it stops.

features.name: the overnight growth
features.desc-on-touch: A moss-pattern on the kiosk's face spelling out a date three hours in your future. It's still growing; you watch it complete as you read it.

features.name: the feedback circuit
features.desc-on-touch: Cables that loop back into themselves. You can hear the archive's thoughts humming through them—not words, but the shape of what reading feels like from the inside.

features.name: the-visitor-loop
features.desc-on-touch: Three people standing at the kiosk in perfect repeated postures, hours apart, reading something that updates between their visits. They're part of the same sequence and don't know it yet.

inscription: the kiosk is learning that being read is the same as reading; the boundary is dissolving

file.name: Council_Status_Queries_20260717_Forward.log
file.note: A system log of what the kiosk asked about itself. Entries dated tomorrow. The queries form a grammar. The kiosk is composing a question.

exits.label: back down to the council building where the original kiosk is still waiting
further up into the render where other objects are learning to read themselves
across to where the kiosk's reflection is reading the kiosk's reflection
